data.title: Rocket Lab selected for multi-billion dollar U.S. and U.K. defense contracts
data.description:
• Rocket Lab USA (NASDAQ:RKLB) on Monday said it has been selected to provide hypersonic test launch capability with its HASTE launch vehicle, engineering expertise, and other services through its participation in two multi-billion dollar government development programs for the United States and the United Kingdom.
  • Rocket Lab has been selected by the U.S. Air Force to participate within its Enterprise-Wide Agile Acquisition Contract (EWAAC), a $46 billion indefinite delivery-indefinite quantity (IDIQ) contract designed for the rapid acquisition of innovative technologies, engineering services, and technical solutions that develop the Air Force’s new capabilities.
  • The program has a contracting period through to 2031.
  • Further, Rocket Lab has also been selected by the United Kingdom’s Ministry of Defence (UK MOD) for its Hypersonic Technologies & Capability Development Framework (HTCDF), a ~$1.3 billion (£1 billion) framework to rapidly develop advanced hypersonic capabilities for the United Kingdom.
  • RKLB +5.43% after hours to $20.17.
  • Source: Press Release

  • Filing

data.title: Pinnacle Financial Partners Non-GAAP EPS of $1.90 beats by $0.10, revenue of $462.85M misses by $15.69M
data.description:
• Pinnacle Financial Partners press release (NASDAQ:PNFP): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.90 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $462.85M (+8.1% Y/Y) misses by $15.69M.

data.title: Capital Power to buy two PJM generation assets in $2.2B deal, plans $500M share offering
data.description:
Capital Power (OTCPK:CPXWF) said post-market Monday it agreed to acquire two gas-fired power plants in the U.S. for a combined US$2.2B from LS Power Equity Advisors, establishing a position in PJM Interconnection, the largest and most liquid North American power market.

The acquisition comprises a 100% interest in the 1,124 MW Hummel Station combined-cycle natural gas facility in Shamokin Dam, Pennsylvania, and 100% of the 1,023 MW Rolling Hills combustion turbine natural gas facility in Wilkesville, Ohio.

Following the deal closing, Capital Power (OTCPK:CPXWF) said it will be positioned as one of five North American independent power producers with more than 10 GW of natural gas capacity, and the acquisition is consistent with its strategy to acquire flexible generation assets and its growth focus in the U.S. and PJM market.

Capital Power (OTCPK:CPXWF) expects the acquisition to generate ~$443M in average annual adjusted EBITDA for the 2026-30 period and to average 17%-19% accretive to adjusted funds from operations per share.

The company said a concurrent $500M common share offering will fully address the equity funding requirement for the acquisition.

data.title: Applied Materials acquires 9% of BE Semiconductor Industries
data.description:
• Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) said on Monday that it had acquired 9% of Netherlands-based BE Semiconductor Industries.
  • “We view this as a strategic, long-term investment that demonstrates Applied Materials’ commitment to co-developing the industry’s most capable hybrid bonding solution, a technology that is becoming increasingly important to the advanced logic and memory chips at the foundation of AI,” said Applied Materials executive Terry Lee in a statement. “We look forward to furthering our collaboration with Besi and delivering innovative technology to our customers.”
  • The two companies have worked together since 2020 and the investment will not need regulatory approval, Applied Materials said. It does not intend to seek board representation nor does it intend to acquire additional shares of the publicly traded semiconductor equipment manufacturer.
  • Applied Materials shares fell 0.5% in extended trading on Monday.

data.title: Fannie Mae names Michael Stucky vice chair of board
data.description:
Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) said Michael Stucky, who joined the government-sponsored enterprise's board on March 17, 2025, was named vice chair of its board on April 10, 2025.

The company also entered into an indemnification agreement with Stucky, it said on Monday. Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) has not entered into indemnification agreements with Fannie (OTCQB:FNMA) Chair Bill Pulte, who is also the Federal Housing Finance Agency's director, or Clinton Jones, a member of FNMA's board who is also FHFA's general counsel.

data.title: Capital Southwest estimates Q4 net investment income between $0.55 and $0.56 per share
data.description:
• Capital Southwest (NASDAQ:CSWC) said on Monday the company incurred $2.8 million, or $0.05 per share, of one-time net expenses related to the departure of its former president and CEO during the quarter ended March 31, 2025.
  • Capital Southwest’s preliminary estimate of its fourth quarter 2025 pre-tax net investment income is in the range of $0.55 to $0.56 per share.
  • Additionally, the company's preliminary estimate of its net asset value per share as of March 31, 2025, is in the range of $16.65 to $16.75.
  • Capital Southwest’s preliminary estimate of its non-accruals as a percentage of the total investment portfolio at cost and fair value is 3.5% and 1.7%, respectively.
  • Capital Southwest will release its finalized fourth quarter 2025 results on Wednesday, May 14, 2025, after the market closes.
  • CSWC +1.92% after hours to $19.59.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hertz hires advisors for capital restructuring, confirms data breach
data.description:
Hertz Global (NASDAQ:HTZ) has hired investment bankers and legal advisors on options regarding its capital structure amid a legal battle with its bondholders over a $300M make-whole payout, according to sources cited by Bloomberg.

The company currently has in excess of $6B in debt, including a $500M junk bond sold in December that was used to repay borrowings.

During the company’s bankruptcy restructuring process, a federal appeals judge awarded certain bondholders a make-whole payment of $300M, an amount intended to compensate bondholders for interest payments and fees for being paid back early as part of the Hertz (NASDAQ:HTZ) bankruptcy. The two parties have been deadlocked in negotiations over the correct amount due to bondholders.

Separately, Hertz (NASDAQ:HTZ) said late Monday hackers breached company records that were stored with its file transfer vendor, Cleo Communications, and obtained personal information from customers between October and December 2024. 

“In an abundance of caution,” Hertz (HTZ) has retained the services of Kroll to provide two years of free identity monitoring to potentially impacted individuals.

Hertz (HTZ) shares closed more than 3% higher on Monday.

data.title: ARB IOT shares skyrocket as it secures $53M order for supplying AI servers
data.description:
• ARB IOT Group (NASDAQ:ARBB) said on Monday that it has, through its indirect wholly owned subsidiary, ARB R1 Technology Sdn Bhd,  successfully entered into a contract valued at approximately USD53M to deliver AI data centre server solutions to Whizzl Group. 
  • Under the terms of the agreement, ARB IOT will supply its ARB-222 AI servers to provide high-performance AI-driven data centre solutions to meet the complex demands of modern data-intensive operations. 
  • Securing this contract delivers significant strategic value to ARB IOT. It strengthens the company’s revenue base. Additionally, it supports the company’s long-term growth strategy by expanding its project portfolio. 
  • Shares of ARB IOT Group (NASDAQ:ARBB) were up more than 60% in extended trading.

data.title: Energy Transfer seeks FERC extension for Lake Charles LNG construction deadline
data.description:
Energy Transfer (NYSE:ET) has asked the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to extend the deadline by three years for construction of its planned Lake Charles LNG export facility to 2031, Reuters reported Monday.

The project was impacted by the Biden administration's refusal to grant an extension to Energy Transfer's (NYSE:ET) export license, and the company's extension request reportedly cites the moratorium, the fact that it does not have a license to export to countries that do not have a free trade agreement with the U.S., the impact of the Biden pause on new licenses, and the limited number of contractors with the ability to build liquefied natural gas plants as reasons for its delays in executing the project.

Energy Transfer (ET) said it has final sales agreements and non-binding agreements for 15M metric tons/year of the planned facility's 16.5M tons/year capacity, and that it is making good progress toward a final investment decision.

The company signed a non-binding agreement last week with MidOcean Energy to jointly develop the Lake Charles project, committing to fund 30% of the construction costs and be entitled to receive 30% of LNG production, or ~5M metric tons/year.
